message:
feat: add fast-path config --show and --json output when no packages installed

When `pi config --show` or `pi config --json` is invoked and no
extension packages are installed, bypass the full async runtime
startup and render config output synchronously via the existing
build_config_report() infrastructure. This mirrors the existing
fast-path for `pi config --paths` and avoids the ~50ms tokio
initialization overhead for a read-only config display operation.

New functions:
- handle_config_show_fast(): Builds and prints the human-readable
  config report with full detail (show=true flag).
- handle_config_json_fast(): Builds the config report and serializes
  it as pretty-printed JSON to stdout.

The fast path is only taken when PackageManager::list_packages_blocking()
returns an empty list, ensuring that package-contributed config sections
still route through the full async path where package config merging
occurs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
